svg.text: Real-World Eviction Implementations — Redis, Caffeine, Memcached
Production architectures, trade-offs, and measured performance metrics
Note: K3 might not be the true
LRU key -- just the oldest in sample
Redis Data Structure — dict (Hash Table) + 24-bit Idle Counter per Key
- LFU: 8-bit frequency + 8-bit decay time (when policy is LFU)
true LRU on typical web workloads
- No pointer chasing, better CPU cache
- Can evict hot keys if unlucky sample
Admission Gate: Window victim competes with probation tail
- Periodic reset needed for CMS decay
3. Memcached — Slab Class LRU with Tail Repair
